CSE5313CSE5313 Coding and information theory for data science (Lecture 19)

CSE5313 Coding and information theory for data science (Lecture 19)

Private information retrieval

Problem setup

Premise:

  • Database X={x1,…,xm}X = \{x_1, \ldots, x_m\}X={x1​,…,xm​}, each xi∈Fqkx_i \in \mathbb{F}_q^kxi​∈Fqk​ is a “file” (e.g., medical record).
  • XXX is coded X↦{y1,…,yn}X \mapsto \{y_1, \ldots, y_n\}X↦{y1​,…,yn​}, yjy_jyj​ stored at server jjj.
  • The user (physician) wants xix_ixi​.
  • The user sends a query qj∼Qjq_j \sim Q_jqj​∼Qj​ to server jjj.
  • Server jjj responds with aj∼Aja_j \sim A_jaj​∼Aj​.

Decodability:

  • The user can retrieve the file: H(Xi∣A1,…,An)=0H(X_i | A_1, \ldots, A_n) = 0H(Xi​∣A1​,…,An​)=0.

Privacy:

  • iii is seen as i∼U=Umi \sim U = U_{m}i∼U=Um​, reflecting server’s lack of knowledge.
  • iii must be kept private: I(Qj;U)=0I(Q_j; U) = 0I(Qj​;U)=0 for all j∈nj \in nj∈n.

In short, we want to retrieve xix_ixi​ from the servers without revealing iii to the servers.

Private information retrieval from Replicated Databases

Simple case, one server

Say n=1,y1=Xn = 1, y_1 = Xn=1,y1​=X.

  • All data is stored in one server.
  • Simple solution:
  • q1=q_1 =q1​= “send everything”.
  • a1=y1=Xa_1 = y_1 = Xa1​=y1​=X.

Theorem: Information Theoretic PIR with n=1n = 1n=1 can only be achieved by downloading the entire database.

  • Can we do better if n>1n > 1n>1?

Collusion parameter

Key question for n>1n > 1n>1: Can servers collude?

  • I.e., does server jjj see any QℓQ_\ellQℓ​, ℓ≠j\ell \neq jℓ=j?
  • Key assumption:
    • Privacy parameter zzz.
    • At most zzz servers can collude.
    • z=1  ⟹  z = 1\impliesz=1⟹ No collusion.
  • Requirement for z=1z = 1z=1: I(Qj;U)=0I(Q_j; U) = 0I(Qj​;U)=0 for all j∈nj \in nj∈n.
  • Requirement for a general zzz:
    • I(QT;U)=0I(Q_\mathcal{T}; U) = 0I(QT​;U)=0 for all T∈n\mathcal{T} \in nT∈n, ∣T∣≤z|\mathcal{T}| \leq z∣T∣≤z, where QT=QℓQ_\mathcal{T} = Q_\ellQT​=Qℓ​ for all ℓ∈T\ell \in \mathcal{T}ℓ∈T.
  • Motivation:
    • Interception of communication links.
    • Data breaches.

Other assumptions:

  • Computational Private information retrieval (even all the servers are hacked, still cannot get the information -> solve np-hard problem):
  • Non-zero MI

Private information retrieval from 2-replicated databases

First PIR protocol: Chor et al. FOCS ‘95.

  • The data X={x1,…,xm}X = \{x_1, \ldots, x_m\}X={x1​,…,xm​} is replicated on two servers.
    • z=1z = 1z=1, i.e., no collusion.
  • Protocol: User has i∼Umi \sim U_{m}i∼Um​.
    • User generates r∼UFqmr \sim U_{\mathbb{F}_q^m}r∼UFqm​​.
    • q1=r,q2=r+eiq_1 = r, q_2 = r + e_iq1​=r,q2​=r+ei​ (ei∈Fqme_i \in \mathbb{F}_q^mei​∈Fqm​ is the iii-th unit vector, q2q_2q2​ is equivalent to one-time pad encryption of xix_ixi​ with key rrr).
    • aj=qjX⊤=∑ℓ∈mqj,ℓxℓa_j = q_j X^\top = \sum_{\ell \in m} q_j, \ell x_\ellaj​=qj​X⊤=∑ℓ∈m​qj​,ℓxℓ​
    • Linear combination of the files according to the query vector qjq_jqj​.
  • Decoding?
    • a2−a1=q2−q1X⊤=eiX⊤=xia_2 - a_1 = q_2 - q_1 X^\top = e_i X^\top = x_ia2​−a1​=q2​−q1​X⊤=ei​X⊤=xi​.
  • Download?
    • aj=a_j =aj​= size of file   ⟹  \implies⟹ downloading twice the size of the file.
  • Privacy?
    • Since z=1z = 1z=1, need to show I(U;Qi)=0I(U; Q_i) = 0I(U;Qi​)=0.
      • I(U;Q1)=I(eU;F)=0I(U; Q_1) = I(e_U; F) = 0I(U;Q1​)=I(eU​;F)=0 since UUU and FFF are independent.
      • I(U;Q2)=I(eU;F+eU)=0I(U; Q_2) = I(e_U; F + e_U) = 0I(U;Q2​)=I(eU​;F+eU​)=0 since this is one-time pad!
Parameters and notations in PIR

Parameters of the system:

  • n=n =n= # servers (as in storage).
  • m=m =m= # files.
  • k=k =k= size of each file (as in storage).
  • z=z =z= max. collusion (as in secret sharing).
  • t=t =t= # of answers required to obtain xix_ixi​ (as in secret sharing).
    • n−tn - tn−t servers are “stragglers”, i.e., might not respond.

Figures of merit:

  • PIR-rate = #\## desired symbols / #\## downloaded symbols
  • PIR-capacity = largest possible rate.

Notaional conventions:

-The dataset X={xj}j∈m={xj,ℓ}(j,ℓ)∈[m]×[k]X = \{x_j\}_{j \in m} = \{x_{j, \ell}\}_{(j, \ell) \in [m] \times [k]}X={xj​}j∈m​={xj,ℓ​}(j,ℓ)∈[m]×[k]​ is seen as a vector in Fqmk\mathbb{F}_q^{mk}Fqmk​.

  • Index Fqmk\mathbb{F}_q^{mk}Fqmk​ using [m]×[k][m] \times [k][m]×[k], i.e., xj,ℓx_{j, \ell}xj,ℓ​ is the ℓ\ellℓ-th symbol of the jjj-th file.

Private information retrieval from 4-replicated databases

Consider n=4n = 4n=4 replicated servers, file size k=2k = 2k=2, collusion z=1z = 1z=1.

Protocol: User has i∼Umi \sim U_{m}i∼Um​.

  • Fix distinct nonzero α1,…,α4∈Fq\alpha_1, \ldots, \alpha_4 \in \mathbb{F}_qα1​,…,α4​∈Fq​.
  • Choose r∼UFq2mr \sim U_{\mathbb{F}_q^{2m}}r∼UFq2m​​.
  • User sends qj=ei,1+αjei,2+αj2rq_j = e_{i, 1} + \alpha_j e_{i, 2} + \alpha_j^2 rqj​=ei,1​+αj​ei,2​+αj2​r to each server jjj.
  • Server jjj responds with aj=qjX⊤=ei,1X⊤+αjei,2X⊤+αj2rX⊤a_j = q_j X^\top = e_{i, 1} X^\top + \alpha_j e_{i, 2} X^\top + \alpha_j^2 r X^\topaj​=qj​X⊤=ei,1​X⊤+αj​ei,2​X⊤+αj2​rX⊤
    • This is an evaluation at αj\alpha_jαj​ of the polynomial fi(w)=xi,1+xi,2⋅w+r⋅w2f_i(w) = x_{i, 1} + x_{i, 2} \cdot w + r \cdot w^2fi​(w)=xi,1​+xi,2​⋅w+r⋅w2.
    • Where rrr is some random combination of the entries of XXX.
  • Decoding?
    • Any 3 responses suffice to interpolate fif_ifi​ and obtain xi=xi,1,xi,2x_i = x_{i, 1}, x_{i, 2}xi​=xi,1​,xi,2​.
    •   ⟹  t=3\implies t = 3⟹t=3, (one straggler is allowed)
  • Privacy?
    • Does qj=ei,1+αjei,2+αj2rq_j = e_{i, 1} + \alpha_j e_{i, 2} + \alpha_j^2 rqj​=ei,1​+αj​ei,2​+αj2​r look familiar?
    • This is a share in ramp scheme with vector messages m1=ei,1,m2=ei,2,mi∈Fq2mm_1 = e_{i, 1}, m_2 = e_{i, 2}, m_i \in \mathbb{F}_q^{2m}m1​=ei,1​,m2​=ei,2​,mi​∈Fq2m​.
    • This is equivalent to 2m2m2m “parallel” ramp scheme over Fq\mathbb{F}_qFq​.
    • Each one reveals nothing to any z=1z = 1z=1 shareholders   ⟹  \implies⟹ Private!

Private information retrieval from general replicated databases

nnn servers, mmm files, file size kkk, X∈FqmkX \in \mathbb{F}_q^{mk}X∈Fqmk​.

Server decodes xix_ixi​ from any ttt responses.

Any ≤z\leq z≤z servers might collude to infer iii (z<tz < tz<t).

Protocol: User has i∼Umi \sim U_{m}i∼Um​.

  • User chooses r1,…,rz∼UFqmkr_1, \ldots, r_z \sim U_{\mathbb{F}_q^{mk}}r1​,…,rz​∼UFqmk​​.
  • User sends qj=∑ℓ=1kei,ℓαjℓ−1+∑ℓ=1zrℓαjk+ℓ−1q_j = \sum_{\ell=1}^k e_{i, \ell} \alpha_j^{\ell-1} + \sum_{\ell=1}^z r_\ell \alpha_j^{k+\ell-1}qj​=∑ℓ=1k​ei,ℓ​αjℓ−1​+∑ℓ=1z​rℓ​αjk+ℓ−1​ to each server jjj.
  • Server jjj responds with aj=qjX⊤=fi(αj)a_j = q_j X^\top = f_i(\alpha_j)aj​=qj​X⊤=fi​(αj​).
    • fi(w)=∑ℓ=1kei,ℓX⊤wℓ−1+∑ℓ=1zrℓX⊤wk+ℓ−1f_i(w) = \sum_{\ell=1}^k e_{i, \ell} X^\top w^{\ell-1} + \sum_{\ell=1}^z r_\ell X^\top w^{k+\ell-1}fi​(w)=∑ℓ=1k​ei,ℓ​X⊤wℓ−1+∑ℓ=1z​rℓ​X⊤wk+ℓ−1 (random combinations of XXX).
    • Caveat: must have t=k+zt = k + zt=k+z.
    •   ⟹  deg⁡fi=k+z−1=t−1\implies \deg f_i = k + z - 1 = t - 1⟹degfi​=k+z−1=t−1.
  • Decoding?
    • Interpolation from any ttt evaluations of fif_ifi​.
  • Privacy?
    • Against any z=t−kz = t - kz=t−k colluding servers, immediate from the proof of the ramp scheme.

PIR-rate?

  • Each aja_jaj​ is a single field element.
  • Download t=k+zt = k + zt=k+z elements in Fq\mathbb{F}_qFq​ in order to obtain xi∈Fqkx_i \in \mathbb{F}_q^kxi​∈Fqk​.
  •   ⟹  \implies⟹ PIR-rate = kk+z=kt\frac{k}{k+z} = \frac{k}{t}k+zk​=tk​.

Theorem: PIR-capacity for general replicated databases

The PIR-capacity for nnn replicated databases with zzz colluding servers, n−tn - tn−t unresponsive servers, and mmm files is C=1−zt1−(zt)mC = \frac{1-\frac{z}{t}}{1-(\frac{z}{t})^m}C=1−(tz​)m1−tz​​.

  • When m→∞m \to \inftym→∞, C→1−zt=t−zt=ktC \to 1 - \frac{z}{t} = \frac{t-z}{t} = \frac{k}{t}C→1−tz​=tt−z​=tk​.
  • The above scheme achieves PIR-capacity as m→∞m \to \inftym→∞

Private information retrieval from coded databases

Problem setup:

Example:

  • n=3n = 3n=3 servers, mmm files xjx_jxj​, xj=xj,1,xj,2x_j = x_{j, 1}, x_{j, 2}xj​=xj,1​,xj,2​, k=2k = 2k=2, and q=2q = 2q=2.
  • Code each file with a parity code: xj,1,xj,2↦xj,1,xj,2,xj,1+xj,2x_{j, 1}, x_{j, 2} \mapsto x_{j, 1}, x_{j, 2}, x_{j, 1} + x_{j, 2}xj,1​,xj,2​↦xj,1​,xj,2​,xj,1​+xj,2​.
  • Server j∈3j \in 3j∈3 stores all jjj-th symbols of all coded files.

Queries, answers, decoding, and privacy must be tailored for the code at hand.

With respect to a code CCC and parameters n,k,t,zn, k, t, zn,k,t,z, such scheme is called coded-PIR.

  • The content for server jjj is denoted by cj=cj,1,…,cj,mc_j = c_{j, 1}, \ldots, c_{j, m}cj​=cj,1​,…,cj,m​.
  • CCC is usually an MDS code.

Private information retrieval from parity-check codes

Example:

Say z=1z = 1z=1 (no collusion).

  • Protocol: User has i∼Umi \sim U_{m}i∼Um​.
  • User chooses r1,r2∼UF2mr_1, r_2 \sim U_{\mathbb{F}_2^m}r1​,r2​∼UF2m​​.
  • Two queries to each server:
    • q1,1=r1+eiq_{1, 1} = r_1 + e_iq1,1​=r1​+ei​, q1,2=r2q_{1, 2} = r_2q1,2​=r2​.
    • q2,1=r1q_{2, 1} = r_1q2,1​=r1​, q2,2=r2+eiq_{2, 2} = r_2 + e_iq2,2​=r2​+ei​.
    • q3,1=r1q_{3, 1} = r_1q3,1​=r1​, q3,2=r2q_{3, 2} = r_2q3,2​=r2​.
  • Server jjj responds with qj,1cj⊤q_{j, 1} c_j^\topqj,1​cj⊤​ and qj,2cj⊤q_{j, 2} c_j^\topqj,2​cj⊤​.
  • Decoding?
    • q1,1c1⊤+q2,1c2⊤+q3,1c3⊤=r1c1+c2+c3+eic1⊤=r1⋅0⊤+xi,1=xi,1q_{1, 1} c_1^\top + q_{2, 1} c_2^\top + q_{3, 1} c_3^\top = r_1 c_1 + c_2 + c_3 + e_i c_1^\top = r_1 \cdot 0^\top + x_{i, 1} = x_{i, 1}q1,1​c1⊤​+q2,1​c2⊤​+q3,1​c3⊤​=r1​c1​+c2​+c3​+ei​c1⊤​=r1​⋅0⊤+xi,1​=xi,1​.
    • q1,1c1⊤+q2,1c2⊤+q3,1c3⊤=r1⋅0⊤+xi,1=xi,1q_{1, 1} c_1^\top + q_{2, 1} c_2^\top + q_{3, 1} c_3^\top = r_1 \cdot 0^\top + x_{i, 1} = x_{i, 1}q1,1​c1⊤​+q2,1​c2⊤​+q3,1​c3⊤​=r1​⋅0⊤+xi,1​=xi,1​.
    • q1,2c1⊤+q2,2c2⊤+q3,2c3⊤=r2c1+c2+c3⊤+eic2⊤=xi,2q_{1, 2} c_1^\top + q_{2, 2} c_2^\top + q_{3, 2} c_3^\top = r_2 c_1 + c_2 + c_3^\top + e_i c_2^\top = x_{i, 2}q1,2​c1⊤​+q2,2​c2⊤​+q3,2​c3⊤​=r2​c1​+c2​+c3⊤​+ei​c2⊤​=xi,2​.
  • Privacy?
    • Every server sees two uniformly random vectors in F2m\mathbb{F}_2^mF2m​.

Proof from coding-theoretic interpretation

Let G=g1⊤,g2⊤,g3⊤G = g_1^\top, g_2^\top, g_3^\topG=g1⊤​,g2⊤​,g3⊤​ be the generator matrix.

  • For every file xj=xj,1,xj,2x_j = x_{j, 1}, x_{j, 2}xj​=xj,1​,xj,2​ we encode xjG=(xj,1g1⊤,xj,2g2⊤,xj,1g3⊤)=(cj,1,cj,2,cj,3)x_j G = (x_{j, 1} g_1^\top, x_{j, 2} g_2^\top, x_{j, 1} g_3^\top) = (c_{j, 1}, c_{j, 2}, c_{j, 3})xj​G=(xj,1​g1⊤​,xj,2​g2⊤​,xj,1​g3⊤​)=(cj,1​,cj,2​,cj,3​).

  • Server jjj stores Xgj⊤=(x1⊤,…,xm⊤)⊤gj⊤=(cj,1,…,cj,m)⊤X g_j^\top = (x_1^\top, \ldots, x_m^\top)^\top g_j^\top = (c_{j, 1}, \ldots, c_{j, m})^\topXgj⊤​=(x1⊤​,…,xm⊤​)⊤gj⊤​=(cj,1​,…,cj,m​)⊤.

  • By multiplying by r1r_1r1​, the servers together store a codeword in CCC:

    • r1Xg1⊤,r1Xg2⊤,r1Xg3⊤=r1XGr_1 X g_1^\top, r_1 X g_2^\top, r_1 X g_3^\top = r_1 X Gr1​Xg1⊤​,r1​Xg2⊤​,r1​Xg3⊤​=r1​XG.

  • By replacing one of the r1r_1r1​’s by r1+eir_1 + e_ir1​+ei​, we introduce an error in that entry:

    • ((r1+ei)Xg1⊤,r1Xg2⊤,r1Xg3⊤)=r1XG+(eiXg1⊤,0,0)\left((r_1 + e_i) X g_1^\top, r_1 X g_2^\top, r_1 X g_3^\top\right) = r_1 X G + (e_i X g_1^\top, 0,0)((r1​+ei​)Xg1⊤​,r1​Xg2⊤​,r1​Xg3⊤​)=r1​XG+(ei​Xg1⊤​,0,0).

  • Downloading this “erroneous” word from the servers and multiply by H=h1⊤,h2⊤,h3⊤H = h_1^\top, h_2^\top, h_3^\topH=h1⊤​,h2⊤​,h3⊤​ be the parity-check matrix.

((r1+ei)Xg1⊤,r1Xg2⊤,r1Xg3⊤)H⊤=(r1XG+(eiXg1⊤,0,0))H⊤=r1XGH⊤+(eiXg1⊤,0,0)H⊤=0+xi,1g1⊤=xi,1.\begin{aligned} \left((r_1 + e_i) X g_1^\top, r_1 X g_2^\top, r_1 X g_3^\top\right) H^\top &= \left(r_1 X G + (e_i X g_1^\top, 0,0)\right) H^\top \\ &= r_1 X G H^\top + (e_i X g_1^\top, 0,0) H^\top \\ &= 0 + x_{i, 1} g_1^\top \\ &= x_{i, 1}. \end{aligned}((r1​+ei​)Xg1⊤​,r1​Xg2⊤​,r1​Xg3⊤​)H⊤​=(r1​XG+(ei​Xg1⊤​,0,0))H⊤=r1​XGH⊤+(ei​Xg1⊤​,0,0)H⊤=0+xi,1​g1⊤​=xi,1​.​

In homework we will show tha this work with any MDS code (z=1z=1z=1).

  • Say we obtained xi,1g1⊤,…,xi,kgk⊤x_{i, 1} g_1^\top, \ldots, x_{i, k} g_k^\topxi,1​g1⊤​,…,xi,k​gk⊤​ (𝑑 − 1 at a time, how?).
  • xi,1g1⊤,…,xi,kgk⊤=xi,Bx_{i, 1} g_1^\top, \ldots, x_{i, k} g_k^\top = x_{i, B}xi,1​g1⊤​,…,xi,k​gk⊤​=xi,B​, where BBB is a k×kk \times kk×k submatrix of GGG.
  • BBB is a k×kk \times kk×k submatrix of GGG   ⟹  \implies⟹ invertible!   ⟹  \implies⟹ Obtain xix_{i}xi​.
Tip

error + known location   ⟹  \implies⟹ erasure. d=2  ⟹  d = 2 \impliesd=2⟹ 1 erasure is correctable.
